title.en: Treatise on the Southwestern Yi people
title.raw: 《西南夷列傳》 Treatise on the Southwestern Yi people

sentences.zh: 西南夷君長以什數，夜郎最大；

translations.literal: The chieftains of the southwestern Yi numbered in the tens; Yelang was the largest.
translations.idiomatic: Among the chieftains of the southwestern Yi, who numbered in the dozens, Yelang was the most powerful.

sentences.zh: 其西靡莫之屬以什數，滇最大；

translations.literal: To its west, those belonging to Mimo numbered in the tens; Dian was the largest.
translations.idiomatic: To the west, the peoples affiliated with Mimo also numbered in the dozens, with Dian being the greatest among them.

sentences.zh: 自滇以北君長以什數，邛都最大：此皆魋結，耕田，有邑聚。

translations.literal: North of Dian, chieftains numbered in the tens; Qiongdu was the largest. All of these wore mallet-shaped chignons, tilled the fields, and had towns and settlements.
translations.idiomatic: North of Dian, the chieftains again numbered in the dozens, with Qiongdu the most prominent. All these peoples wore their hair in topknots, cultivated the fields, and lived in towns and settlements.

sentences.zh: 其外西自同師以東，北至楪榆，名為巂、昆明，皆編發，隨畜遷徙，毋常處，毋君長，地方可數千里。

translations.literal: Beyond them, from Tongshi in the west to the east, and north to Dieyu, those called Sui and Kunming all braided their hair, migrated with their herds, had no fixed dwellings and no chieftains, and their territory extended for several thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: Beyond these settled peoples, stretching from Tongshi in the west eastward and north to Dieyu, were the tribes known as Sui and Kunming. They braided their hair, followed their herds from pasture to pasture, had no permanent settlements and no chieftains, and their lands stretched across several thousand li.

sentences.zh: 自冄以東北，君長以什數，徙、筰都最大；

translations.literal: Northeast of Ran, chieftains numbered in the tens; Xi and Zuodu were the largest.
translations.idiomatic: To the northeast of Ran, the chieftains numbered in the dozens, with Xi and Zuodu the most powerful among them.

sentences.zh: 自筰以東北，君長以什數，冄駹最大。

translations.literal: Northeast of Zuo, chieftains numbered in the tens; Ranmang was the largest.
translations.idiomatic: Farther to the northeast of Zuo, the chieftains again numbered in the dozens, with Ranmang the greatest.

sentences.zh: 其俗或士箸，或移徙，在蜀之西。

translations.literal: Their customs were some sedentary and some nomadic; they lived to the west of Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Some of these peoples were settled while others were nomadic, and they all lived to the west of Shu.

sentences.zh: 自冄駹以東北，君長以什數，白馬最大，皆氐類也。

translations.literal: Northeast of Ranmang, chieftains numbered in the tens; Baima was the largest, and all were of the Di people.
translations.idiomatic: Northeast of Ranmang, the chieftains numbered in the dozens, with Baima the most prominent. All of them belonged to the Di people.

sentences.zh: 此皆巴蜀西南外蠻夷也。

translations.literal: These were all the barbarian peoples beyond the southwest of Ba and Shu.
translations.idiomatic: All of these were the outlying barbarian peoples beyond the southwest of Ba and Shu.

sentences.zh: 始楚威王時，使將軍莊蹻將兵循江上，略巴、黔中以西。

translations.literal: In the time of King Wei of Chu, he sent General Zhuang Qiao to lead troops up the Yangtze, conquering the areas west of Ba and Qianzhong.
translations.idiomatic: In the reign of King Wei of Chu, he dispatched General Zhuang Qiao to lead an army up along the Yangtze River, subjugating the lands west of Ba and Qianzhong.

sentences.zh: 莊蹻者，故楚莊王苗裔也。

translations.literal: Zhuang Qiao was a descendant of King Zhuang of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuang Qiao was himself a descendant of King Zhuang of Chu.

sentences.zh: 蹻至滇池，地方三百里，旁平地，肥饒數千里，以兵威定屬楚。

translations.literal: Qiao reached Dian Lake, an area of three hundred li, with flat land alongside it fertile for thousands of li, and by military force brought it under Chu's dominion.
translations.idiomatic: When Qiao reached Dian Lake, he found a region spanning three hundred li, flanked by flat, fertile land stretching for thousands of li. He subdued the area by force of arms and claimed it for Chu.

sentences.zh: 欲歸報，會秦擊奪楚巴、黔中郡，道塞不通，因還，以其眾王滇，變服，從其俗，以長之。

translations.literal: He wished to return and report, but at that time Qin attacked and seized Chu's Ba and Qianzhong commanderies, blocking the roads. So he turned back, made himself king of Dian with his followers, changed his dress, adopted local customs, and ruled over them.
translations.idiomatic: He intended to return and report his success, but Qin had meanwhile seized Chu's commanderies of Ba and Qianzhong, cutting off his route home. Left with no choice, he turned back, proclaimed himself king of Dian at the head of his followers, adopted the local dress and customs, and governed the people.

sentences.zh: 秦時常頞略通五尺道，諸此國頗置吏焉。

translations.literal: During the Qin dynasty, Chang E roughly opened the Five-Foot Road, and officials were installed in various states of the region.
translations.idiomatic: During the Qin dynasty, the official Chang E opened a rough path known as the Five-Foot Road, and officials were stationed among the various states of the region.

sentences.zh: 十餘歲，秦滅。

translations.literal: After more than ten years, Qin fell.
translations.idiomatic: A little over a decade later, the Qin dynasty collapsed.

sentences.zh: 及漢興，皆棄此國而開蜀故徼。

translations.literal: When the Han arose, they abandoned all these states and reopened only the old frontier passes of Shu.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han dynasty arose, the court abandoned all these states and simply reopened the old frontier passes of Shu.

sentences.zh: 巴蜀民或竊出商賈，取其筰馬、僰僮、髦牛，以此巴蜀殷富。

translations.literal: Some people of Ba and Shu secretly went out to trade, obtaining their Zuo horses, Bo slaves, and yaks, and thus Ba and Shu became rich.
translations.idiomatic: Merchants from Ba and Shu would slip across the frontier to trade, bringing back Zuo horses, Bo slaves, and yaks, and through this commerce the people of Ba and Shu grew prosperous.

sentences.zh: 建元六年，大行王恢擊東越，東越殺王郢以報。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Jianyuan, the Grand Herald Wang Hui attacked Eastern Yue, and Eastern Yue killed King Ying in response.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of the Jianyuan era, the Grand Herald Wang Hui attacked the Eastern Yue, and the Eastern Yue killed their king, Ying, and submitted his head in response.

sentences.zh: 恢因兵威使番陽令唐蒙風指曉南越。

translations.literal: Hui, taking advantage of the military momentum, sent the Prefect of Fanyang, Tang Meng, to convey hints and make things known to the Southern Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Riding the momentum of this campaign, Hui dispatched Tang Meng, the Prefect of Fanyang, to deliver veiled warnings to the Southern Yue.

sentences.zh: 南越食蒙蜀枸醬，蒙問所從來，曰「道西北牂柯，牂柯江廣數里，出番禺城下」。

translations.literal: The Southern Yue served Meng Shu citrus sauce. When Meng asked where it came from, they said, 'It comes via the northwest through Zangke; the Zangke River is several li wide and flows out below the city of Panyu.'
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Yue served Tang Meng a citrus sauce from Shu. When he asked where it came from, they told him, 'It comes by way of Zangke to the northwest. The Zangke River is several li across and flows down to the walls of Panyu.'

sentences.zh: 蒙歸至長安，問蜀賈人，賈人曰：「獨蜀出枸醬，多持竊出市夜郎。

translations.literal: When Meng returned to Chang'an, he asked Shu merchants, and the merchants said: 'Only Shu produces citrus sauce. Many carry it out secretly to trade in Yelang.
translations.idiomatic: When Tang Meng returned to Chang'an, he questioned merchants from Shu. They told him, 'Only Shu produces this citrus sauce. Traders smuggle large quantities of it out to sell in Yelang.

sentences.zh: 夜郎者，臨牂柯江，江廣百餘步，足以行船。

translations.literal: Yelang borders the Zangke River, which is over a hundred paces wide, enough to navigate boats.
translations.idiomatic: Yelang sits along the Zangke River, which is over a hundred paces wide — more than enough to sail boats upon.

sentences.zh: 南越以財物役屬夜郎，西至同師，然亦不能臣使也。」

translations.literal: Southern Yue uses wealth and goods to command the allegiance of Yelang, westward to Tongshi, but it still cannot truly make them its subjects.'
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Yue uses its wealth to command the allegiance of Yelang and the peoples westward to Tongshi, but even so it cannot truly bring them to heel as subjects.'

sentences.zh: 蒙乃上書說上曰：「南越王黃屋左纛，地東西萬餘里，名為外臣，實一州主也。

translations.literal: Meng then submitted a memorial persuading the Emperor, saying: 'The King of Southern Yue rides under a yellow canopy with a left-side banner, his territory stretches over ten thousand li from east to west; he is nominally an outer vassal, but in reality he is the master of an entire province.
translations.idiomatic: Tang Meng then submitted a memorial to the Emperor, arguing, 'The King of Southern Yue rides beneath a yellow canopy with a great banner at his left, and his domains stretch more than ten thousand li from east to west. In name he is an outer vassal, but in truth he is the lord of an entire province.

sentences.zh: 今以長沙、豫章往，水道多絕，難行。

translations.literal: Now, going by way of Changsha and Yuzhang, the waterways are mostly impassable and difficult to travel.
translations.idiomatic: At present, approaching by way of Changsha and Yuzhang, the waterways are frequently impassable and the route extremely difficult.

sentences.zh: 竊聞夜郎所有精兵，可得十餘萬，浮船牂柯江，出其不意，此制越一奇也。

translations.literal: I have heard that Yelang possesses elite troops that could number over a hundred thousand. If we float boats down the Zangke River and strike by surprise, this would be a masterstroke for subduing Yue.
translations.idiomatic: I have learned that Yelang commands elite troops numbering over a hundred thousand. Were we to launch boats down the Zangke River and strike without warning, it would be a masterstroke for subduing the Yue.

sentences.zh: 誠以漢之彊，巴蜀之饒，通夜郎道，為置吏，易甚。」

translations.literal: Truly, with the strength of Han and the wealth of Ba and Shu, opening a road through Yelang and installing officials there would be extremely easy.'
translations.idiomatic: With the might of Han and the riches of Ba and Shu behind us, opening a road through Yelang and installing officials there would be the simplest of undertakings.'

sentences.zh: 上許之。

translations.literal: The Emperor approved it.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor approved the plan.

sentences.zh: 乃拜蒙為郎中將，將千人，食重萬餘人，從巴蜀筰關入，遂見夜郎侯多同。

translations.literal: He then appointed Meng as a Palace Gentleman General, leading a thousand soldiers and over ten thousand supply personnel, entering through the Zuo Pass from Ba and Shu, and thereupon met with the Marquis of Yelang, Duotong.
translations.idiomatic: Tang Meng was appointed Palace Gentleman General and given command of a thousand soldiers with over ten thousand supply personnel. He entered through the Zuo Pass from Ba and Shu and secured an audience with Duotong, the Marquis of Yelang.

sentences.zh: 蒙厚賜，喻以威德，約為置吏，使其子為令。

translations.literal: Meng gave generous gifts, explained Han's might and virtue, agreed to install officials, and had the marquis's son appointed as a magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Tang Meng lavished gifts upon him, impressed upon him the might and benevolence of Han, and negotiated an agreement to install officials, with the marquis's own son appointed as a local magistrate.

sentences.zh: 夜郎旁小邑皆貪漢繒帛，以為漢道險，終不能有也，乃且聽蒙約。

translations.literal: The small settlements beside Yelang all coveted Han silks, but believed the roads from Han were too perilous for Han to ever control them, so they tentatively agreed to Meng's terms.
translations.idiomatic: The smaller settlements surrounding Yelang all coveted Han silks, yet they believed that the treacherous roads would forever prevent Han from exerting real control over them. For the time being, they agreed to Tang Meng's terms.

sentences.zh: 還報，乃以為犍為郡。

translations.literal: When he returned and reported, the area was made into the Commandery of Jianwei.
translations.idiomatic: When Tang Meng returned and made his report, the court established the Commandery of Jianwei.

sentences.zh: 發巴蜀卒治道，自僰道指牂柯江。

translations.literal: Troops from Ba and Shu were mobilized to build a road from Bodao pointing toward the Zangke River.
translations.idiomatic: Soldiers from Ba and Shu were conscripted to construct a road from Bodao toward the Zangke River.

sentences.zh: 蜀人司馬相如亦言西夷邛、筰可置郡。

translations.literal: Sima Xiangru of Shu also said that commanderies could be established in the western Yi lands of Qiong and Zuo.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru, a man of Shu, likewise argued that commanderies could be established in the western Yi territories of Qiong and Zuo.

sentences.zh: 使相如以郎中將往喻，皆如南夷，為置一都尉，十餘縣，屬蜀。

translations.literal: Xiangru was sent as a Palace Gentleman General to persuade them, and all was arranged as with the southern Yi: a commandant and over ten counties were established, subordinate to Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru was dispatched as a Palace Gentleman General to win them over. The arrangements followed the same pattern as with the southern Yi: a commandant was appointed, more than ten counties were established, and all were placed under the jurisdiction of Shu.

sentences.zh: 當是時，巴蜀四郡通西南夷道，戍轉相馕。

translations.literal: At this time, four commanderies of Ba and Shu were opening roads to the southwestern Yi, with garrisons supplying provisions to one another in relay.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, four commanderies of Ba and Shu were engaged in opening roads to the southwestern Yi, with garrisons relaying provisions from one to the next.

sentences.zh: 數歲，道不通，士罷餓離溼死者甚眾；

translations.literal: After several years the roads remained impassable, and very many soldiers died of exhaustion, starvation, and diseases from the damp climate.
translations.idiomatic: After several years the roads were still impassable, and great numbers of soldiers perished from exhaustion, starvation, and the fevers of the damp climate.

sentences.zh: 西南夷又數反，發兵興擊，秏費無功。

translations.literal: The southwestern Yi also repeatedly revolted; troops were sent to attack them, wasting resources with no success.
translations.idiomatic: The southwestern Yi also rose in revolt repeatedly. Troops were dispatched to suppress them, but the campaigns only squandered resources without result.

sentences.zh: 上患之，使公孫弘往視問焉。

translations.literal: The Emperor was troubled by this and sent Gongsun Hong to go inspect and inquire about the situation.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor grew alarmed and sent Gongsun Hong to investigate the situation firsthand.

sentences.zh: 還對，言其不便。

translations.literal: When he returned and reported, he said it was not advantageous.
translations.idiomatic: Upon his return, Gongsun Hong reported that the enterprise was ill-advised.

sentences.zh: 及弘為御史大夫，是時方筑朔方以據河逐胡，弘因數言西南夷害，可且罷，專力事匈奴。

translations.literal: When Hong became Imperial Secretary, at that time the court was building Shuofang to hold the Yellow River and drive out the Hu. Hong therefore repeatedly spoke of the harm of the southwestern Yi campaign, saying it should be suspended so that efforts could be concentrated against the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: When Gongsun Hong rose to the position of Imperial Secretary, the court was then building the fortress of Shuofang to command the Yellow River and drive back the Hu. He repeatedly argued that the southwestern Yi campaign was doing more harm than good and ought to be suspended so the court could concentrate its strength against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 上罷西夷，獨置南夷夜郎兩縣一都尉，稍令犍為自葆就。

translations.literal: The Emperor abandoned the western Yi, retaining only two counties and one commandant for the southern Yi and Yelang, and gradually let Jianwei sustain itself on its own.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor abandoned the western Yi campaign, retaining only two counties and a single commandant for the southern Yi and Yelang, and allowed the Commandery of Jianwei to consolidate on its own.

sentences.zh: 及元狩元年，博望侯張騫使大夏來，言居大夏時見蜀布、邛竹、杖，使問所從來，曰「從東南身毒國，可數千里，得蜀賈人市」。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanshou, the Marquis of Bowang, Zhang Qian, returned from his mission to Daxia. He said that while in Daxia he had seen Shu cloth, Qiong bamboo, and walking sticks. When he asked where they came from, the reply was: 'From the southeast, from the kingdom of Shendu, several thousand li away, where they are obtained from Shu merchants at market.'
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the Yuanshou era, Zhang Qian, the Marquis of Bowang, returned from his mission to Daxia. He reported that while in Daxia he had seen cloth from Shu, Qiong bamboo, and walking sticks. When he inquired where they came from, the people told him, 'From the kingdom of Shendu to the southeast, several thousand li away, where they are purchased from merchants of Shu.'

sentences.zh: 或聞邛西可二千里有身毒國。

translations.literal: It was also heard that about two thousand li west of Qiong there was the kingdom of Shendu.
translations.idiomatic: It was further reported that some two thousand li west of Qiong lay the kingdom of Shendu.

sentences.zh: 騫因盛言大夏在漢西南，慕中國，患匈奴隔其道，誠通蜀，身毒國道便近，有利無害。

translations.literal: Qian therefore eagerly argued that Daxia lay to the southwest of Han, admired China, and was troubled that the Xiongnu blocked its road. If the route through Shu were truly opened, the road through Shendu would be convenient and close, with benefits and no harm.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qian pressed his case with enthusiasm: Daxia lay to the southwest of Han and admired the Middle Kingdom, but was frustrated that the Xiongnu blocked the way between them. If a route through Shu could be opened, the road through Shendu would be far shorter and more convenient — offering nothing but advantage.

sentences.zh: 於是天子乃令王然于、柏始昌、呂越人等，使閒出西夷西，指求身毒國。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Emperor ordered Wang Ranyu, Bo Shichang, Lyu Yueren, and others to be sent secretly through the western Yi lands toward the west, in search of a route to the kingdom of Shendu.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor thereupon ordered Wang Ranyu, Bo Shichang, Lu Yueren, and others to slip through the western Yi territories and press westward in search of the kingdom of Shendu.

sentences.zh: 至滇，滇王嘗羌乃留，為求道西十餘輩。

translations.literal: When they reached Dian, the King of Dian, Chang Qiang, detained them and sent over ten parties to seek a route to the west on their behalf.
translations.idiomatic: When the envoys reached Dian, the King of Dian, Chang Qiang, detained them and dispatched more than ten parties to find a westward route on their behalf.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，皆閉昆明，莫能通身毒國。

translations.literal: After more than a year, all were blocked by the Kunming people, and none could get through to Shendu.
translations.idiomatic: After more than a year, every party was blocked by the Kunming people, and none managed to reach Shendu.

sentences.zh: 滇王與漢使者言曰：「漢孰與我大？」

translations.literal: The King of Dian said to the Han envoys: 'Which is greater, Han or my kingdom?'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Dian asked the Han envoys, 'Which is greater — Han, or my kingdom?'

sentences.zh: 及夜郎侯亦然。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Yelang also said the same.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Yelang asked the very same question.

sentences.zh: 以道不通故，各自以為一州主，不知漢廣大。

translations.literal: Because the roads were impassable, each considered himself the lord of an entire province and did not know how vast Han was.
translations.idiomatic: Because the roads between them were impassable, each ruler fancied himself the lord of an entire province, with no notion of how vast the Han empire truly was.

sentences.zh: 使者還，因盛言滇大國，足事親附。

translations.literal: When the envoys returned, they enthusiastically reported that Dian was a great state, worthy of being made a close ally.
translations.idiomatic: When the envoys returned, they spoke with great enthusiasm of Dian as a powerful state well worth cultivating as an ally.

sentences.zh: 天子注意焉。

translations.literal: The Emperor took note of this.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor took careful note of their report.

sentences.zh: 及至南越反，上使馳義侯因犍為發南夷兵。

translations.literal: When the Southern Yue rebelled, the Emperor sent the Marquis of Chiyi to mobilize southern Yi troops through Jianwei.
translations.idiomatic: When the Southern Yue rose in rebellion, the Emperor sent the Marquis of Chiyi to muster southern Yi troops through Jianwei.

sentences.zh: 且蘭君恐遠行，旁國虜其老弱，乃與其眾反，殺使者及犍為太守。

translations.literal: The lord of Qielan feared that if he marched far away, neighboring states would seize his old and weak. So he and his people revolted, killing the envoy and the Grand Administrator of Jianwei.
translations.idiomatic: The lord of Qielan feared that if he marched his warriors far from home, neighboring states would prey upon the elderly and weak left behind. He and his people therefore revolted, killing the Han envoy and the Grand Administrator of Jianwei.

sentences.zh: 漢乃發巴蜀罪人嘗擊南越者八校尉擊破之。

translations.literal: Han then sent convicts from Ba and Shu along with eight colonels who had previously fought against the Southern Yue to attack and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: The Han court then mobilized convicts from Ba and Shu along with eight colonels who had served in the campaign against the Southern Yue, and together they crushed the rebellion.

sentences.zh: 會越已破，漢八校尉不下，即引兵還，行誅頭蘭。

translations.literal: As the Yue had already been defeated, the eight Han colonels did not advance further south. Instead they withdrew their troops and along the way punished Toulan.
translations.idiomatic: Since the Yue had already been defeated, the eight Han colonels did not press farther south. Instead they turned their army around and, on the return march, punished Toulan.

sentences.zh: 頭蘭，常隔滇道者也。

translations.literal: Toulan had always been the one blocking the road to Dian.
translations.idiomatic: Toulan was the power that had long blocked the road to Dian.

sentences.zh: 已平頭蘭，遂平南夷為牂柯郡。

translations.literal: After pacifying Toulan, they then pacified the southern Yi and made it the Commandery of Zangke.
translations.idiomatic: Once Toulan was pacified, the remaining southern Yi were subdued and their lands were organized into the Commandery of Zangke.

sentences.zh: 夜郎侯始倚南越，南越已滅，會還誅反者，夜郎遂入朝。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Yelang had originally relied on the Southern Yue. With the Southern Yue now destroyed, and the returning army punishing the rebels, Yelang submitted and came to court.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Yelang had long relied upon the Southern Yue for support. Now that the Southern Yue were destroyed and the returning army had punished the rebels, Yelang submitted and presented himself at court.

sentences.zh: 上以為夜郎王。

translations.literal: The Emperor made him King of Yelang.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor bestowed upon him the title of King of Yelang.

sentences.zh: 南越破後，及漢誅且蘭、邛君，并殺筰侯，冄駹皆振恐，請臣置吏。

translations.literal: After the Southern Yue were defeated, when Han punished Qielan and the lord of Qiong, and also killed the Marquis of Zuo, the Ranmang were all terrified and requested to submit as vassals and have officials installed.
translations.idiomatic: After the fall of the Southern Yue, when Han punished Qielan and the lord of Qiong and put the Marquis of Zuo to death, the Ranmang were all seized with terror. They requested permission to submit as vassals and have Han officials installed among them.

sentences.zh: 乃以邛都為越巂郡，筰都為沈犁郡，冄駹為汶山郡，廣漢西白馬為武都郡。

translations.literal: Qiongdu was made the Commandery of Yuesui, Zuodu the Commandery of Shenli, Ranmang the Commandery of Wenshan, and Baima in western Guanghan the Commandery of Wudu.
translations.idiomatic: Qiongdu was reorganized as the Commandery of Yuesui, Zuodu as the Commandery of Shenli, Ranmang as the Commandery of Wenshan, and Baima in western Guanghan as the Commandery of Wudu.

sentences.zh: 上使王然于以越破及誅南夷兵威風喻滇王入朝。

translations.literal: The Emperor sent Wang Ranyu to use the military prestige from the defeat of the Yue and the punishment of the southern Yi to persuade the King of Dian to come to court.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor dispatched Wang Ranyu to leverage the military prestige won by the defeat of the Yue and the punishment of the southern Yi, and to persuade the King of Dian to present himself at court.

sentences.zh: 滇王者，其眾數萬人，其旁東北有勞寖、靡莫，皆同姓相扶，未肯聽。

translations.literal: The King of Dian had tens of thousands of subjects. To his northeast were Laojin and Mimo, all of the same surname and mutually supportive, and they were not willing to comply.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Dian commanded tens of thousands of subjects. To his northeast lay Laojin and Mimo, peoples of the same clan who supported one another, and they refused to comply.

sentences.zh: 勞寖、靡莫數侵犯使者吏卒。

translations.literal: Laojin and Mimo repeatedly attacked and harassed the envoys, officials, and soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: Laojin and Mimo repeatedly attacked the Han envoys, officials, and soldiers.

sentences.zh: 元封二年，天子發巴蜀兵擊滅勞寖、靡莫，以兵臨滇。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yuanfeng, the Emperor sent troops from Ba and Shu to attack and destroy Laojin and Mimo, and brought military force to bear upon Dian.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of the Yuanfeng era, the Emperor dispatched troops from Ba and Shu to annihilate Laojin and Mimo, then turned his army upon Dian.

sentences.zh: 滇王始首善，以故弗誅。

translations.literal: The King of Dian had originally been the first to show goodwill, and for this reason he was not put to death.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Dian had been the first among the southwestern rulers to show goodwill, and for this reason his life was spared.

sentences.zh: 滇王離難西南夷，舉國降，請置吏入朝。

translations.literal: The King of Dian separated himself from the troubles of the southwestern Yi, surrendered his entire kingdom, and requested that officials be installed and that he be allowed to come to court.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Dian distanced himself from the unrest of the southwestern Yi, surrendered his entire kingdom, and requested that Han officials be installed and that he be permitted to present himself at court.

sentences.zh: 於是以為益州郡，賜滇王王印，復長其民。

translations.literal: Thereupon the area was made the Commandery of Yizhou, the King of Dian was granted a royal seal, and he was allowed to continue governing his people.
translations.idiomatic: The territory was thereupon organized as the Commandery of Yizhou. The King of Dian was granted a royal seal and permitted to continue governing his people.

sentences.zh: 西南夷君長以百數，獨夜郎、滇受王印。

translations.literal: Among the southwestern Yi chieftains, who numbered in the hundreds, only Yelang and Dian received royal seals.
translations.idiomatic: Of the hundreds of chieftains among the southwestern Yi, only Yelang and Dian received the honor of a royal seal.

sentences.zh: 滇小邑，最寵焉。

translations.literal: Dian was a small state, yet received the most imperial favor.
translations.idiomatic: Dian was but a small state, yet it was the most favored of all.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：楚之先豈有天祿哉？

translations.literal: The Grand Historian remarks: Did the ancestors of Chu possess Heaven's blessing?
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: Did the ancestors of Chu truly enjoy the mandate of Heaven?

sentences.zh: 在周為文王師，封楚。

translations.literal: During the Zhou, they served as tutors to King Wen and were enfeoffed as Chu.
translations.idiomatic: In the days of Zhou, their forebear served as tutor to King Wen and was enfeoffed as the lord of Chu.

sentences.zh: 及周之衰，地稱五千里。

translations.literal: By the decline of Zhou, their territory was said to extend five thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: By the time of Zhou's decline, their territory was said to stretch five thousand li.

sentences.zh: 秦滅諸候，唯楚苗裔尚有滇王。

translations.literal: When Qin destroyed the feudal lords, only among the descendants of Chu did the King of Dian still endure.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin destroyed the feudal lords, only among the descendants of Chu did the kingship of Dian endure.

sentences.zh: 漢誅西南夷，國多滅矣，唯滇復為寵王。

translations.literal: When Han punished the southwestern Yi, many states were destroyed, yet only Dian was again made a favored king.
translations.idiomatic: When Han subjugated the southwestern Yi and destroyed many of their states, only Dian was once again honored with the rank of king.

sentences.zh: 然南夷之端，見枸醬番禺，大夏杖、邛竹。

translations.literal: Yet the beginning of the southern Yi affair came from seeing citrus sauce in Panyu and walking sticks and Qiong bamboo in Daxia.
translations.idiomatic: Yet the whole affair of the southern Yi began with the chance discovery of citrus sauce in Panyu and of walking sticks and Qiong bamboo in Daxia.

sentences.zh: 西夷后揃，剽分二方，卒為七郡。

translations.literal: The western Yi were gradually trimmed, the marauders were divided into two regions, and ultimately seven commanderies were established.
translations.idiomatic: The western Yi were gradually subdued, their raiding peoples split between two fronts, and in the end seven commanderies were carved from their lands.
